meta.purpose: Ideas and future work not yet queued. Low commitment, high optionality.
meta.usage: Move items to tasks.json backlog when ready to work on them.

icebox.description: Dedicated PM agent that runs at review thresholds to audit task health
icebox.notes: Should check for stale tasks, missing context, priority drift, orphaned dependencies. Triggered by modification_count crossing a threshold.

icebox.description: Script to render the task DAG as a dependency graph
icebox.notes: Could output DOT format for Graphviz or a simple ASCII tree. Useful for spotting bottlenecks and orphaned chains.

icebox.description: Set up LiteLLM or similar proxy for model routing across local and cloud backends
icebox.notes: Agents should be able to specify model requirements (capability level, latency, cost) and have requests routed to the appropriate backend. Supports swapping models without changing agent code.

icebox.description: Explore using a small local model as the stack manager process
icebox.notes: The stack manager reads/writes/validates task state -- doesn't need deep reasoning. Could run on a lightweight local model, freeing expensive models for substantive work. Test whether a small model (e.g., Qwen 0.6B) can reliably manage JSON task files.

icebox.description: Implement the audio-only overseer interaction mode
icebox.notes: A constant voice loop with the orchestrator agent. STT via local model, LLM for processing, lightweight TTS for responses. Should work hands-free, no screen required. Like a phone call with an executive assistant who can write code.

icebox.description: Evaluate LiveKit for the voice communication layer
icebox.notes: Could enable calling in from a phone to talk to agents. Handles the audio transport, leaving STT/TTS to the local pipeline.

icebox.description: Track API spending per agent and per task
icebox.notes: Finance department concept from the vision. Should support budget limits so the developer isn't bothered for every penny but spending doesn't run away. Attribution of costs to specific PRs or work items.

icebox.description: Queue async tasks for overnight local execution
icebox.notes: Take advantage of the M1/M3 Max running 24/7. Queue lower-priority tasks (security reviews, code quality sweeps, research) for overnight batch processing when the developer isn't actively working.

icebox.description: Python CLI tool for agents to monitor and debug CI builds
icebox.notes: Subcommands: smart-monitor (poll until build finishes), debug (fetch full console output), list-failures. Wraps CircleCI or GitHub Actions API. Should not block the conversational thread -- run in background.

icebox.description: Consider an MCP server interface for the task management system
icebox.notes: Could provide faster read/write to task state than git-commit-per-edit. Agents interact with tasks via MCP tool calls. Git commits happen at a coarser granularity for semantic state changes.

icebox.description: Agent that reviews text for AI writing tells and enforces natural prose style
icebox.notes: Should stay updated on current AI writing patterns to avoid. Reviews documentation before it's committed. Checks for overused words, formulaic structure, excessive hedging, etc.

icebox.description: Script to set up a fresh Mac with the full development environment
icebox.notes: Install Homebrew, OrbStack, pyenv, Oh-My-Zsh, copy dotfiles, set up Claude Code with agent definitions, configure Super Whisper. Should be idempotent -- safe to re-run.

icebox.description: Evaluate TOML as an alternative to JSON for human-readable structured data
icebox.notes: Flagged as personally interesting -- TOML may be visually easier to parse than JSON for configuration files. Investigate whether task files or config could benefit from TOML format.

icebox.description: Periodic summary of engineering activity for the developer
icebox.notes: CEO report concept: what was worked on, PRs merged, test results, blockers. Runs on a schedule. Helps the developer stay oriented without reading every commit.

icebox.description: Explore physical gesture input via wearable ring device
icebox.notes: Electric ring band for enter, escape, mode switching. Bimodal interaction: voice + gestures. No magic voice command words -- gestures handle the discrete actions.

icebox.description: Share a single STT model instance between Super Whisper and the agentic system
icebox.notes: Avoid loading the same NVIDIA Parakeet model twice. Super Whisper and the agent voice pipeline should be able to share one instance to save memory.

icebox.description: Investigate KV cache sharing between agents using the same local model
icebox.notes: When batching tokens from different agents, leverage KV caching for shared context prefixes. Could significantly reduce inference cost for agents with similar system prompts.

icebox.description: Automatic agent spawning on CI failure
icebox.notes: When a CI build fails, automatically create an agent to investigate. One agent per branch (not per build). Deduplication logic to avoid spawning redundant investigators.

icebox.description: Periodic automated security review of the codebase
icebox.notes: Configurable frequency. Dependency scanning, code review for common vulnerabilities, OWASP checks. Runs as a background agent on a schedule.

icebox.description: Distinct TTS voices for different agent roles
icebox.notes: The developer should be able to tell by ear which agent is speaking. Orchestrator, researcher, test runner each get a different voice.

icebox.description: Structured capability self-assessment for agents
icebox.notes: Ask agents what they need to be effective. An employment interview pattern: what tools do you need, what are your limitations, what would make you better at this role.

icebox.description: Wire up external human user testing via APIs
icebox.notes: Services like usertesting.com. An agent should be able to commission a few humans to try something and report back.

icebox.description: Call in from a phone to interact with agents
icebox.notes: Via LiveKit cloud or similar. Queue tasks, check progress, have a conversation with the orchestrator from a mobile device.

icebox.description: Evaluate database with constraints as task state backend
icebox.notes: JSON files are the starting point, but a database could enforce constraints more naturally. Worth considering if the file-based approach hits scaling limits. Keep files as the interchange format.

icebox.description: MCP server connecting agents to OmniFocus for real-world task management
icebox.notes: A local agent with appropriate privacy tagging could interact with personal task management. Bridges the gap between coding tasks and real-world todos.

icebox.description: Validation at configurable frequencies based on computational cost
icebox.notes: Cheap structural checks run on every commit. Expensive semantic checks run less often. Full architecture reviews run on a schedule. Each tier has its own frequency.

icebox.description: Define branching strategy for multi-agent parallel work
icebox.notes: Stack management on main like a heartbeat. Feature work on branches. Cross-merging strategy for agents working in parallel. Needs thought about conflict resolution.
